description: Day 2 schema additions. Extends project.schema.json v1.1.0 (676 lines, 14 node types). Adds 4 new node types, extends 3 existing types, adds 1 new shared definition block, adds blueprint system, adds tuning config.

1.2.0.notes: building_workflow replaces and supersedes the previous per-building crafting approach. All crafting buildings (forge, apothecary, library, mine, farm, barracks) use this single node type.

building_upgrade->building[cross].compiler_check: referenced building must exist in graph and have sufficient max_level

x-formula-variable-registry.description: Every variable available inside any formula string across the schema. The engine populates all of these before evaluating any formula. Designer can reference any of these by name.

item_quality_tier.source: consumable items only. Set at craft time. 0=base, 1=superior, 2=masterwork, 3=legendary

item_craft_cost_{resource_id}.source: item definition. Replace {resource_id} with actual id e.g. item_craft_cost_iron

item_base_stat_{stat}.source: item definition. Replace {stat} with stat name e.g. item_base_stat_atk

worker_skill.source: assigned artisan primary_stat value. 0 if slot unassigned

worker_specialization_match.source: 1 if assigned artisan specialization matches workflow action_type, else 0

worker_level.source: assigned artisan hero level. 1 if unassigned

momentum.source: library-type buildings only. Current momentum value

streak_count.source: apothecary-type buildings. Consecutive same-recipe job count

resource_{id}.source: current resource pool amount. Replace {id} with resource id

forge_level.source: alias for building_level. Kept for readability in forge formulas

output_rule.description: One entry in an output_rules array. Covers all five output types.

output_type.description: resource: adds to resource pool. item: adds to hero/guild inventory. consumable: adds to buff stockpile. world_effect: mutates world state directly. hero_instance: creates a new recruitable hero.

target.description: For resource/item/consumable: the id of the resource or item node. For world_effect: the id of the node to affect. For hero_instance: omit, use target_class instead.

target_class.description: hero_instance only. The hero_class id to instantiate.

yield_formula.description: resource only. Formula string. Variables from x-formula-variable-registry.

quantity.description: item/consumable. Fixed quantity produced per job.

modifier_target.description: apply_modifier only. The stat or node property to modify.

modifier_value.description: apply_modifier only. The multiplier or addend.

stat_roll.description: hero_instance only. How stats are rolled within the rarity tier.

success_table.description: RNG resolution model for a workflow. Roll order: failure first, then crit, then normal. This means crits only fire on non-failures.

base_failure.description: Starting failure probability before formula modifiers. Set 0.0 for buildings where failure makes no sense (barracks).

base_crit.description: Starting crit probability before formula modifiers.

failure_chance_formula.description: Overrides base_failure if present. Formula resolving to 0.0-1.0. Use max(0, ...) to floor at zero.

crit_behavior.description: double_output: quantity ×crit_multiplier. quality_upgrade: item output becomes crit_output_item instead. rarity_upgrade: hero_instance rarity bumped one tier. breakthrough: fires breakthrough_table in addition to normal output. extend_duration: consumable buff duration ×crit_multiplier.

crit_output_item.description: quality_upgrade only. The item id produced on crit instead of the base output item.

breakthrough_table.description: breakthrough only. Weighted pool of bonus world_effects that fire on crit in addition to normal output.

failure_behavior.description: consume_inputs_no_output: forge default. partial_refund: apothecary default, returns 50% of inputs. reset_progress_refund_inputs: library default, resets tick progress, returns all inputs.

failure_refund_rate.description: partial_refund only. Fraction of inputs returned.

failure_grants_xp.description: Whether the artisan gains XP on failure. Recommended true — learning from mistakes.

failure_xp_multiplier.description: XP multiplier applied on failure. 0.5 = half XP.

momentum_config.description: Research momentum system. Library-type buildings. Rewards continuous operation. Decays on idle.

gain_per_job.description: Momentum points added on each completed job.

decay_per_idle_tick.description: Momentum lost per tick when no job is processing.

thresholds.description: Ascending momentum values that trigger flow states.

speed_bonus.description: Duration reduction as a fraction. 0.25 = 25% faster.

crit_bonus.description: Added to crit_chance at this threshold.

streak_bonus.description: Concentration system. Apothecary-type buildings. Rewards same-recipe repetition.

threshold.description: Consecutive same-recipe jobs needed to activate.

duration_reduction.description: Duration reduction fraction while streak is active. 0.20 = 20% faster.

crit_bonus.description: Added to crit_chance while streak is active.

label.description: UI label shown during streak e.g. 'Focused Brewing'.

buff_config.description: Consumable buff definition. Used when item_type is consumable.

apply_scope.description: party: all heroes benefit. hero: applied to one specific hero slot. hero_class: only heroes of matching class.

apply_target.description: hero_class only. The hero_class id that receives the buff.

duration_type.description: expedition_count: consumed after N expeditions regardless of outcome. expedition_success: only decrements on success. permanent_until_death: consumed on hero death.

duration_value.description: Number of expeditions the buff lasts.

stat.description: The stat to affect e.g. atk, def, spd, lck, xp_gain, loot_bonus.

stack_behavior.description: refresh: resets duration to max. extend: adds duration_value to remaining. intensify: stacks effect value up to stack_cap.

stack_cap.description: intensify only. Maximum number of stacks.

buff_slot_cost.description: How many hero buff slots this consumable occupies. Use 2-3 for powerful endgame buffs.

passive_event.description: Time-based event emitter on a building. Used for walk-in recruits (Tavern), random discoveries, etc. Fires independently of the workflow queue.

interval_formula.description: Ticks between event fires. Formula string.

hero_class_pool.description: hero_available only. Class ids that can appear.

available_for_ticks.description: hero_available only. How long the offer persists before expiring.

blueprint_meta.description: Metadata block present on every .blueprint.json file. Enables the blueprint library UI and community sharing.

requires_schema_version.description: Minimum schema version required to import this blueprint.

generator_origin.description: Present if blueprint was produced or adapted by the generator pipeline.

building_workflow.description: Defines one action a crafting building can perform. Generic — works for forge (melt/craft), apothecary (brew/distill), library (research), mine (produce), barracks (recruit), and all future buildings. The engine does not know what 'melt' means — it executes the configured behavior against the formula context.

behavior.description: Engine behavior class. consume_item: takes an item from inventory as input. consume_resource: takes resources. produce_resource: no input, passive output. modify_item: alters an existing item in-place (enchanter). recruit_hero: produces a hero_instance.

action_type.description: Designer-facing label for the action. Free string — 'melt', 'brew', 'research', 'mine', 'recruit'. No engine logic attached; used for display and streak_bonus matching.

workflow_mode.description: queued: player explicitly adds jobs. passive: runs continuously while artisan is assigned or building is active, no queue management.

host_building.description: ID of the building node this workflow belongs to.

target_filter.description: consume_item only. Filters which items the player can select for this workflow. null = no item input required.

inputs.description: consume_resource only. Resources consumed per job.

use_item_salvage_profile.description: consume_item + behavior=consume_item only. If true, output_rules are read from the source item's salvage_profile instead of this workflow. Enables one generic melt workflow for all item types.

duration_formula.description: Ticks to complete one job. Formula string. For batch efficiency: 'base_duration * (1 + (batch_size - 1) * 0.3)'

duration_base_ticks.description: Base ticks before formula modifiers. Referenced as 'base_duration' in formula string.

streak_bonus.description: Optional. Apothecary-type buildings. Omit if not needed.

momentum_config.description: Optional. Library-type buildings. Omit if not needed.

xp_on_complete.description: XP granted to assigned artisan on job completion (before failure_xp_multiplier).

total_ticks_required.description: passive mode only. Total accumulated ticks needed to fire output_rules once.

building_prerequisites.description: building_upgrade IDs that must be completed first.

building_upgrade.description: One upgrade tier for a building. Controls slot count, batch size, workflow unlocks, auto-repeat unlocks, and cross-building dependency gates. Displayed in the Upgrade Tree tuning panel.

unlocks_auto_repeat_on.description: building_workflow IDs on which auto_repeat becomes available.

apply_modifier.description: Optional permanent modifier applied to a stat or formula variable when this upgrade completes.

ui_discovery.description: Controls how this upgrade appears before it is unlocked.

cross_building.description: Cross-building prerequisites. Compiler validates these references exist and have sufficient max_level.

crafting_recipe.description: A specific recipe used by a building_workflow with behavior=consume_resource. Defines inputs, outputs, and craft time. Referenced by building_workflow via used_by connection.

required_workflow.description: The building_workflow ID that must be active for this recipe to appear.

success_table_override.description: null = inherits host building_workflow success_table. Set to override per-recipe.

crit_output.description: What happens on crit for this specific recipe.

result_item.description: quality_upgrade only. The upgraded item id.

blueprint.description: A saved subgraph that can be placed on the canvas as a unit. Shipped presets (forge_standard, apothecary_standard, library_standard) and user-created/community-shared blueprints use this same format.

nodes.description: Complete node definitions. IDs will be namespaced on import to prevent collisions.

edges.description: Edge definitions referencing node IDs within this blueprint. Namespaced on import.

canvas_layout.description: x/y positions for each node id. Used to place the subgraph on the canvas at import.

hero_class.description: Additions to the existing hero_class node type from Day 1.

hero_type.description: combat: can go on expeditions. artisan: assigned to buildings, cannot go on expeditions.

combat_eligible.description: Derived from hero_type but explicit for compiler clarity.

primary_stat.description: artisan only. The stat that feeds into worker_skill in formula context. e.g. craft_skill, alchemy_skill, research_skill.

specializations.description: artisan only. Available specialization labels. One is chosen on recruit. Matching action_type on a workflow grants bonus.

building_affinity.description: artisan only. Building IDs where this artisan receives full bonuses. Can be assigned elsewhere at reduced efficiency.

unique_passive.description: Optional passive ability that activates under a defined condition.

recruitment.description: Controls where and how this hero_class can be recruited.

base_tier_rarities.description: Rarities available from the guild roster without prerequisites.

rare_epic_unlock.description: Rare and epic tier recruitment requires this building.

item.description: Additions to the existing item node type from Day 1.

item_type.description: equipment: goes to hero inventory, occupies a slot. material: crafting input, goes to resource pool. consumable: goes to buff stockpile. quest: non-transferable, triggers events. key: unlocks specific content.

salvage_profile.description: What this item yields when melted/salvaged. Read by consume_item workflows with use_item_salvage_profile=true.

stack_max.description: consumable/material only. Maximum stack size in stockpile.

building.description: Additions to the existing building node type from Day 1.

has_workflows.description: True if this building hosts building_workflow nodes. Tells the engine to run processBuildingTick for this building.

artisan_slots.description: Configuration for artisan assignment. Only relevant when has_workflows=true.

buff_slots.description: Hero buff slot configuration. Present on hero management buildings (Barracks, etc.).

passive_events.description: Time-based events fired by this building independently of the workflow queue.

x-tuning-config.description: Configuration for the Tuning utility tab in the editor. Defines danger zone thresholds for the Formula Lab panel.

danger_zones.label: Very short jobs may cause performance issues
Large batches may cause UI lag

x-preset-blueprints.description: The three reference blueprints shipped with the editor. Each is a complete building system ready to drop on the canvas.

new_validation_rules.description: Every building_id in building_upgrade.requires.cross_building must reference an existing building node in the graph.
new_validation_rules.error: building_upgrade '{id}' requires building '{building_id}' which does not exist in the graph.

new_validation_rules.description: The referenced building must have at least one building_upgrade at or above the required level.
new_validation_rules.error: building_upgrade '{id}' requires building '{building_id}' at level {level}, but that building only reaches level {max_level}.

new_validation_rules.description: building_upgrade.requires.cross_building must not form a cycle. e.g. Forge L2 requires Mine L2, Mine L2 requires Forge L2 = deadlock.
new_validation_rules.error: Circular building prerequisite detected: {cycle_path}. This will deadlock the player.

new_validation_rules.description: Every output_rule with output_type=resource must reference an existing resource node.
new_validation_rules.error: building_workflow '{id}' output_rule references resource '{target}' which does not exist in the graph.

new_validation_rules.description: Every output_rule with output_type=item or consumable must reference an existing item node.
new_validation_rules.error: building_workflow '{id}' output_rule references item '{target}' which does not exist in the graph.

new_validation_rules.description: A hero_class with hero_type=artisan must have combat_eligible=false.
new_validation_rules.error: hero_class '{id}' is hero_type=artisan but has combat_eligible=true. Artisans cannot join expeditions.

new_validation_rules.description: On blueprint import, requires_schema_version must be <= current schema version.
new_validation_rules.error: Blueprint '{blueprint_id}' requires schema version {requires_schema_version} but current version is {current_version}. Run migration tool first.
